value.description: Name of the study arm as stated by the authors
Classification of the arm. Waitlist arms are mapped to passive_control. Use null when category cannot be determined.

identifier.description: Composed identifier '<arm name> (<category|unknown>)' used as the 'group' field in measurements. Use the literal string 'unknown' when category.value is null.

value.description: True if this instrument is designated as the primary or co-primary outcome measure of the study

components.description: List of components reported numerically in the paper for this instrument, formatted as '<Instrument> - <Subscale|Total>'. Only include components for which any statistic (mean, std, median, etc.) is reported for at least one (group, time) cell.

instruments.description: Metadata for each questionnaire/instrument used to score participants. Not necessarily restricted to anxiety or depression. Empty array if no instruments are used (e.g., protocol papers without planned instruments listed).

group.description: Identifier matching one entry in arms[].identifier (format: '<arm name> (<category|unknown>)').

primary.description: Mirrors instruments[].primary.value for the parent instrument.

mean.description: Raw arithmetic mean reported for this (group, outcome, time) cell. Null if not reported as a raw mean (e.g., median, adjusted mean, graphical only).

std.description: Standard deviation reported alongside the raw mean. Null if not reported as SD (e.g., SE, CI, IQR, range).

note.description: Free-text observation when mean and/or std are null and a different statistic was reported (e.g., 'Median (IQR) = 12 (8-16)'), or when only an adjusted/LSMean was reported, or when values were only available graphically. Null when mean and std are both reported and no further note is needed.

measurements.description: Long-format list of measurements. One entry per (group, outcome, time) cell for which any numeric statistic is reported. Maps directly to a DataFrame with columns Group | Outcome | primary | mean | std (plus note/pages/quotes for traceability).
